findings.description: Header logo and agency names are repositioned and reduced in prominence in the HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 1 of the PDF, the NC Water Resources logo appears large at top left and the governor/secretary/director names are right-aligned at top right. In the HTML, the logo is smaller and the names are stacked on the left beneath it.

findings.description: Top header text formatting and alignment are not preserved.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows the official names in blue, right-aligned, with role labels in lighter italic styling. The HTML renders them as plain dark text in a left-aligned stacked list, reducing visual hierarchy.

findings.description: First-page spacing and placement differ noticeably from the PDF, especially around the header and title block.
findings.evidence: The PDF centers the MEMORANDUM title higher on the page with broad whitespace and a structured letter-style block below. The HTML places the title lower with a long underline and compresses the surrounding layout.

findings.description: Section heading styling is simplified in the HTML.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses more formal serif styling with bold roman numeral section headers; the HTML uses sans-serif headings and different weight/size treatment, though the headings remain distinct.

findings.description: Bullet/list formatting is less faithfully reproduced.
findings.evidence: On page 2, the PDF bullet list has tighter indentation and serif body formatting; in the HTML the bullets are larger-spaced and more web-styled, though list structure is preserved.

findings.description: Bulleted comment summaries are visually restyled compared with the PDF.
findings.evidence: On pages 3 and 4, the PDF uses serif text with bold underlined topic labels; the HTML preserves bullets and emphasis but with different font, spacing, and a more modern web presentation.

findings.description: Attachment list formatting differs from the original.
findings.evidence: On page 4, the PDF shows a compact numbered list under 'IV. Attachments' with serif text and tight spacing. The HTML presents the same items with larger spacing and sans-serif styling.

findings.description: The HTML introduces fabricated contact information and page footer text not present in the visible PDF pages.
findings.evidence: HTML includes: "State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Resources 1611 Mail service Center | Raleigh, North Carolina 27699-1611 919 707 9000". In the provided PDF images, page 1 footer visibly shows only a small line beginning with "State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Resources" and the mailing/address/phone details are not legible/present in the source image content provided. This added detailed contact block cannot be verified from the PDF and appears invented.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is imperfect because major non-body document header content is not semantically distinguished, and the document contains nested <main> elements.
findings.evidence: HTML uses one h1 ("MEMORANDUM") and h2 for sections I-IV, which is mostly correct, but the government-official masthead text ("PAT MCCRORY", "DONALD R. VAN DER VAART", "S. JAY ZIMMERMAN") is placed in a plain paragraph rather than structured header elements. Also, HTML contains an outer <main> and an inner <main>, which is structurally invalid.

findings.description: One image has empty alt text despite being a visible decorative/footer graphic; alt handling is inconsistent.
findings.evidence: HTML first image has alt="North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality logo" which is accurate. The second image uses alt="". Since the footer graphic is visible in the PDF, the empty alt is acceptable if decorative, but the treatment is inconsistent.

findings.description: Text accuracy issue in attachment reference numbering within Section II.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 states: "A total of 37 individuals completed sign-in forms at the meeting (Attachment I)." But page 4 attachment list shows "1. Public Notice of March 1, 2016 Meeting" and "2. Public Meeting Sign-in Forms". Therefore the sign-in forms correspond to Attachment 2/II, not Attachment I. HTML reproduces the same mismatch: "(Attachment I)" while later listing sign-in forms as item 2. This is a fidelity-preserved source inconsistency, not an HTML conversion error; however, no HTML-specific alteration beyond this was found.
